meaning: adj.爱争吵的,卑下的,一般的
examples: The old lady was still being ornery, but at least she had consented to this visit.
But they seem to be becoming more ornery.
Andres Nocioni: He's not only tough, but also ornery.
First, however ornery the Lib Dems become, their survival instinct binds them into the coalition.
Now, in the darkness, on the side of the mountain, the man seemed ornery and off.
